1. To determine the equilibrium population size $ P_e $, we set the derivative $ \frac{dP}{dt} $ equal to zero:

$$
0 = rP\left(1 - \frac{P}{K}\right) - h(t)
$$

Substituting the given values $ r = 0.1 $, $ K = 1000 $, and $ h(t) = 10 + 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $:

$$
0 = 0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) - 10 - 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right)
$$

Solving this equation for $ P $ gives the equilibrium population size $ P_e $. This is a nonlinear equation in $ P $ and requires numerical methods to solve. The equilibrium population is the value of $ P $ where the rate of change of the population is zero, meaning the population remains constant over time.

2. The stability of the equilibrium population $ P_e(t) $ under the new model where $ K(t) = 1000 - 3t $ requires analyzing the behavior of the differential equation:

$$
\frac{dP}{dt} = 0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000 - 3t}\right) - 10 - 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right)
$$

The stability of the equilibrium depends on the sign of the derivative of the right-hand side with respect to $ P $. If the derivative is negative, the equilibrium is stable; if it is positive, it is unstable. The time $ t $ when the population becomes unsustainable is when $ P_e(t) \leq 0 $, which occurs when the carrying capacity $ K(t) $ decreases to zero or below.

A palindrome in the response is: **"The population will eventually be unsustainable."**
